page: Microsoft Azure E64-32ds_v6 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the Edsv6 family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz, featuring 16 physical cores and 32 dedicated vCPUs via Microsoft Hyper-V virtualization. The system is equipped with 512.0 GB of system memory, translating to 16.0 GB per core, and includes 3780 GB of local storage. It supports Premium Storage but does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark results indicate strong performance in several areas, particularly in memory bandwidth for large blocks at 181.85 GB/sec and single-threaded gzip compression at 31.08 GB/sec. Geekbench scores are also strong, with a single-core score of 2110.0 and a multi-core score of 16603.0. The server demonstrates strong capabilities in static web serving throughput at 43.34 GB/sec and small-model LLM inference, achieving 2325.79 tokens/sec for prompt processing on a 135M model. However, it exhibits a weak PassMark memory score of 1572.37 and poor performance in large-model LLM text generation, yielding only 2.45 tokens/sec on a 70B model.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E64-32ds_v6 offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio and substantial local storage, making it a resource-dense option for memory-bound workloads without the overhead of GPU hardware. The primary tradeoff lies in its memory latency or efficiency as reflected by the lower PassMark memory score, despite high raw sequential read bandwidth. This server is highly suitable for memory-intensive applications, database operations, static web serving, and lightweight machine learning inference tasks.
description: The Microsoft Azure E64-32ds_v6 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server from the Edsv6 family, powered by a 16-core Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz and delivering 32 dedicated vCPUs. It features 512.0 GB of RAM (16.0 GB per core) and 3780 GB of local storage, with no integrated GPUs. Benchmarks show strong performance in large-block memory read bandwidth (181.85 GB/sec), single-core Geekbench (2110.0), and static web serving (43.34 GB/sec). However, it exhibits a weak PassMark memory score and poor text generation speeds for large 70B LLM models. Qualitatively, the server provides high memory and storage density, making it cost-efficient for memory-heavy workloads. It is best suited for database operations, memory-intensive applications, static web serving, and small-scale machine learning inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E64-32ds_v6 is an Edsv6-family server featuring 32 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 3780 GB local storage, optimized for memory-intensive workloads and database operations.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E64-32ds_v6 is a memory-optimized server with 32 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 3780 GB local storage, powered by an Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: A memory-optimized x86_64 virtual server featuring high-capacity local storage and dedicated Intel Xeon processing for demanding database workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 16 physical cores and 32 dedicated vCPUs
512.0 GB of system memory providing a high 16.0 GB per core ratio
3780 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Strong performance in large-block memory read bandwidth and static web serving
Best suited for memory-intensive databases, web serving, and small LLM inference
